Neon Echo.style: Polished synthpop production with bright, punchy drum machines and layered synthesizers. Known for energetic, radio-ready hooks.
Neon Echo.typical_listener: Pop fans who want electronic texture without losing accessibility.
Neon Echo.catalog_notes: Two songs in this catalog: Sunrise City (pop/happy) and Night Drive Loop (synthwave/moody). Distinct moods but consistent production polish.

LoRoom.style: Classic lo-fi hip hop. Warm vinyl crackle, jazz-sampled loops, minimal percussion. Entirely instrumental.
LoRoom.typical_listener: Students, coders, and remote workers who need non-distracting background music.
LoRoom.catalog_notes: Two songs: Midnight Coding (chill) and Focus Flow (focused). Focus Flow has the higher instrumentalness score (0.89) — the go-to for deep work.

Voltline.style: Heavy, guitar-forward rock with aggressive drumming and high dynamic range. No electronic softening.
Voltline.typical_listener: Rock and metal fans looking for intense, high-energy tracks.
Voltline.catalog_notes: One song in catalog: Storm Runner (rock/intense, energy=0.91). Closest substitute for metal in this catalog.

Paper Lanterns.style: Minimal lo-fi with heavy acoustic and ambient leanings. Very low energy, almost meditative.
Paper Lanterns.typical_listener: Listeners who want background music that disappears into the room.
Paper Lanterns.catalog_notes: One song: Library Rain (lofi/chill, energy=0.35, instrumentalness=0.92). Among the most instrumental tracks in the catalog.

Max Pulse.style: High-energy workout and sports pop. Compressed, punchy production designed for physical exertion.
Max Pulse.typical_listener: Gym users and athletes who need motivational energy, not lyrical depth.
Max Pulse.catalog_notes: One song: Gym Hero (pop/intense, energy=0.93). Highest energy pop track in the catalog. Frequently surfaces for high-energy queries even when mood doesn't match.

Orbit Bloom.style: Ambient and space music. Long sustained pads, no percussion, fully instrumental. Designed for immersive listening.
Orbit Bloom.typical_listener: Meditators, yoga practitioners, and background-listening minimalists.
Orbit Bloom.catalog_notes: One song: Spacewalk Thoughts (ambient/chill, energy=0.28, instrumentalness=0.95). One of the lowest-energy tracks in the catalog.

Slow Stereo.style: Vintage jazz with a coffee-shop warmth. Light acoustic guitar, brushed drums, muted trumpet. 2000s-era production feel.
Slow Stereo.typical_listener: Jazz listeners and lofi fans who want acoustic warmth with more harmonic complexity than lofi loops.
Slow Stereo.catalog_notes: One song: Coffee Shop Stories (jazz/relaxed, 2000s era, detail_mood=nostalgic). High liveness score (0.45) gives it a live-room feel.

Indigo Parade.style: Indie pop with bright guitar tones and emotionally direct lyrics. More organic than mainstream pop.
Indigo Parade.typical_listener: Listeners who want pop hooks with a personal, handmade feel.
Indigo Parade.catalog_notes: One song: Rooftop Lights (indie pop/happy, energy=0.76). Frequently the second-choice for pop/happy queries because genre is 'indie pop', not 'pop'.

Flow State.style: Underground hip-hop with raw production and aggressive lyrical delivery. Low mainstream appeal by design.
Flow State.typical_listener: Hip-hop fans who prioritize authenticity and lyrical complexity over chart performance.
Flow State.catalog_notes: One song: Block by Block (hip-hop/motivated, popularity=74, detail_mood=aggressive). Target for users with low target_popularity preferences.

Circuit Breaker.style: Festival EDM. Maximum BPM, hard bass drops, euphoric buildups engineered for large crowd response.
Circuit Breaker.typical_listener: Club and festival goers. Not designed for headphone or home listening.
Circuit Breaker.catalog_notes: One song: Drop Zone (edm/euphoric, energy=0.97, loudness=0.95). Highest energy and loudness track in the entire catalog.

Clara Voss.style: Solo classical piano with a melancholic, introspective tone. Sparse, slow, and fully acoustic.
Clara Voss.typical_listener: Classical listeners and ambient fans who want emotional depth without lyrical content.
Clara Voss.catalog_notes: One song: Autumn Sonata (classical/melancholic, energy=0.22, instrumentalness=0.98). The most acoustic and most instrumental track in the catalog.

Dusty Pines.style: Narrative country-folk. Acoustic guitar, storytelling lyrics, nostalgic production with warm tape sound.
Dusty Pines.typical_listener: Country and Americana fans who value lyrical storytelling over production.
Dusty Pines.catalog_notes: One song: Gravel Road Home (country/nostalgic, 2000s era). Surfaces in all-neutral queries because its mid-range values sit close to 0.5 on most features.

Jasmine Keys.style: Smooth R&B with soulful vocal delivery and groove-based production. Romantic and emotionally warm.
Jasmine Keys.typical_listener: R&B fans and listeners seeking romantic, late-night background music.
Jasmine Keys.catalog_notes: One song: Velvet Morning (r&b/romantic, energy=0.55). The only R&B track in the catalog.

June Sparrow.style: Acoustic folk with a dreamlike quality. Fingerpicked guitar, breathy vocals, organic texture.
June Sparrow.typical_listener: Folk and indie listeners who prefer quiet, intimate listening experiences.
June Sparrow.catalog_notes: One song: Wildflower Lullaby (folk/dreamy, energy=0.30, acousticness=0.91). High liveness (0.35) adds a live-recording intimacy.

Sunroot.style: Reggae with a warm Caribbean groove. Off-beat guitar, heavy bass, and a positive, carefree mood.
Sunroot.typical_listener: Reggae fans and listeners who want feel-good, relaxed music without being sedated.
Sunroot.catalog_notes: One song: Island Echo (reggae/happy, energy=0.58). Only reggae track in the catalog. Surfaces for happy-mood queries when genre constraint is relaxed.

Ray Hollow.style: Late-night blues with a raw, minimalist production. Acoustic guitar, slow tempo, emotionally heavy.
Ray Hollow.typical_listener: Blues fans and late-night listeners who want emotional honesty over production polish.
Ray Hollow.catalog_notes: One song: 3 AM Diner (blues/sad, energy=0.44). Only sad-mood track in the catalog. Surfaces for sad/melancholic queries even when energy doesn't match.
